---
title: R project template, part two
author: 
  - name: novica
    url: "https://discindo.org/authors/novica"
date: '2026-07-12'
categories:
  - R 
  - template
  - project
  - module
  - tools
description: "An opinionated template for modern R projects."
execute:
  eval: false
image: ""
---

[![](https://ko-fi.com/img/githubbutton_sm.svg)](https://ko-fi.com/J3J8133RYV)

In recent months the R world has exploded with many new tools (mostly written in 
`Rust`) that try to solve one or another problem that exists in the ecosystem. I 
am tracking all of these at a dedicated page at [r-devs.com](https://r-devs.com). 
Just a few days ago one of these tools - [uvr](https://github.com/nbafrank/uvr) 
- was released with support for R installation using the portable, relocatable 
builds published by [rstudio/r-builds](https://github.com/rstudio/r-builds). This 
is a major change that brings the R workflow very close to what is possible in 
python and delivering on uvr's promise to "bring uv-style project management to R".

This prompted some rework on the R project template that aims to replicate as close
as possible a python-like workflow for R. I introduced this 
[template](/posts/2026-02-24-r-project-template/) few months ago with the notes
that it works though it could be better.

Now I think a step towards making it better has been achieved by what `uvr` introduced.
Additionally, another tool - [rd2qmd](https://github.com/eitsupi/rd2qmd) - comes
in handy to generate the documentation and pass it on to Quarto for a more streamlined
site generation and deployment.

The `rd2qmd` path has also prompted some ideas to improve `box` internals about 
how it handles documentation, and maybe it will be beneficial to propose these 
changes to the `box` upstream.

The landscape keeps changing though. Posit just recently released another tool 
- [ir](https://github.com/r-lib/ir) - that 
[according to some](https://bsky.app/profile/andrewholz.bsky.social/post/3mq5xhnd3u22j) 
is an 'R version of uv'. Depending on how that develops in the future, I may decide
to update the template to use it. And this is just noting potentially the most
influential tool that was just released, [other tools](https://r-devs.com) 
are out there too.

But the template is ready to be used. 
Code is here: [R project template](https://github.com/novica/r-project-template). 
Try it out.
